items.title: Why online masterclasses are replacing the executive MBA?
items.summary: The traditional Executive MBA is losing its dominance as learning needs change. New platforms offer short, practitioner-led programs for busy professionals. These programs provide direct access to experts who have solved real business problems. This shift reflects how quickly business knowledge now becomes outdated. Indian executives are redefining continuous learning for their evolving careers.

items.title: Eleven Power commits to invest over Rs 4,700 crore for distribution network
items.summary: Eleven Power has proposed investing over ₹4,700 crore to build a renewable-led electricity distribution network in Gurugram and Nuh, after applying for a parallel distribution licence from the Haryana Electricity Regulatory Commission. The company said funding for the first phase is fully secured, but the project depends on regulatory approval.

items.title: Karnataka HC dismisses PIL against 7,400-acre Bidadi township land acquisition
items.summary: In a recent ruling, the High Court dismissed a challenge against land acquisition for a Bengaluru township, reinforcing that it cannot interfere with the government's policy choices. Chief Minister DK Shivakumar refrained from making any statements regarding the verdict. In light of farmer protests, a committee is set to review the proposed project, while the government guarantees that the land status of unwilling farmers will not be affected.

items.title: India's core sector output grows 5% in June under new base year series, hits 5-month high
items.summary: June 2026 marked a notable five percent growth in India's core industries index, with iron ore production soaring to forty-three point nine percent. Significant growth was also observed in cement and electricity. Conversely, natural gas and crude oil outputs faced reductions. For the April-June 2026 timeframe, the total growth measured at three point six percent.

items.title: India says no proposal to offer long-term tax relief for domestic equity investors
items.summary: India's finance ministry confirmed no plan to remove equity LTCG tax. This clarification follows recent tax easing for foreign investors in government debt. Domestic investors will continue paying a 12.5% LTCG tax on qualifying equity gains. The exemption for foreign portfolio investors applies to government securities only. This move aims to attract stable, long-term foreign capital into India.

items.title: Ryanair profits drop as Iran war puts off passengers and lifts fuel costs
items.summary: The Irish airline said oil prices had increased costs meanwhile Brent crude surpassed $90.

items.title: India's net FDI inflows likely to reach $15 bn in FY27
items.summary: India's net FDI inflows are expected to rebound to $15 billion in fiscal year 2027. This rebound will be supported by healthy gross inflows and moderating repatriation outflows. Policy measures and a nuanced FDI framework are also expected to boost foreign investment. Outbound FDI from India is also projected to remain healthy throughout the fiscal year. These improvements signal a positive outlook for India's capital account.

items.title: Global customers are watching closely, says Skyroot founder Pawan Chandana
items.summary: This is a global milestone achieved by an Indian private company, and it demonstrates to the entire ecosystem that building world-class space technology in India is possible, Skyroot founder Pawan Chandana said

items.summary: CuspAI is part of a new wave of companies looking to use AI to solve challenges in the physical world.

items.title: India’s edible oil industry flags 17-fold surge in Nepal imports, seeks policy review
items.summary: In a pressing appeal, the Indian Vegetable Oil Producers' Association urges a swift revision of India's edible oil import regulations. Recent years have seen a surge in duty-free refined oil entering from Nepal, which has moved significant refining operations beyond India's borders, causing negative repercussions for local industries and fiscal health.

items.summary: South Korea's Samsung Biologics said on Monday that it had made an all-cash bid of 1.46 billion Swiss francs ($1.8 billion) for Switzerland's PolyPeptide Group.

items.title: Why Sterling Holidays is moving beyond holiday memberships before its standalone listing
items.summary: Sterling Holiday Resorts Ltd, part of Thomas Cook India, has halted new vacation memberships to concentrate on hotel operations and expand its properties. The firm plans to grow its portfolio to over 95 resorts, boosting its revenue model.

items.summary: Veriqus intends to build a strong presence across Tier II and other high-growth cities as wealth is now being built well beyond the metros.

items.summary: The country's fifth-largest cement maker sells about 1.5 million tonnes of cement annually in Gujarat and expects to take the sales in the state to five million tonnes by 2030.

items.summary: With onion prices rising ahead of the festive and wedding season, the Centre plans a phased release of buffer stocks from September to bridge supply gaps and curb inflation.

items.title: How rural India is bearing the brunt of the inflation rise
items.summary: India’s rural inflation has moved closer to 5%, while the urban rate remains below 4%. The gap is usually explained by higher food prices, but the latest data suggest price pressures may be spreading beyond food.

items.title: From 'Super El Niño' to Europe’s extreme heatwave: How this year's weather shocks are rattling commodity markets
items.summary: Markets are underestimating the impact of climate volatility on a range of asset classes, commodity strategists say.

items.title: A yo-yo monsoon is threatening India's crops and the power grid
items.summary: India's monsoon rainfall is becoming more erratic due to El Niño and global warming, with the shift threatening agricultural output and straining the nation's power grids. Dry spells and sudden downpours have disrupted summer crop sowing and rural incomes. Urban areas are seeing increased power demand for cooling during monsoon pauses.

items.title: Centre may revive NaBFID-IIFCL merger plan after REC-PFC move to build infra financing giant
items.summary: The creation of NaBFID in 2021 came with the ambition to merge it with its older cousin IIFCL, but the plan never took off. Momentum from the ongoing merger of REC and PFC has given the plan a new life. The idea is to create a stronger DFI that can aid India's rapidly growing infra financing needs.

items.title: Higher auto spending, aviation bet will push us to $1 billion: Tata Tech’s Harris
items.summary: Tata Technologies CEO Warren Harris says the firm's $1 billion revenue target by FY28 remains firmly on track, even though it needs a 27% CAGR, six times its recent pace, after a tariff- and EV-driven slowdown.

items.summary: Turtlemint expects to end FY27 profitable despite seasonal quarterly swings, after posting its first-ever profitable quarter in Q4 FY26.

items.title: 'We were fixated on not failing': Skyroot's long road to orbit
items.summary: ‘The unknown unknowns are where we learn,’ says Skyroot Aerospace cofounder and chief executive Pawan Kumar Chandana. India's sole space unicorn chose patience over speed by delaying its maiden commercial rocket launch by about two years, he says, adding that the success has reinforced the strategy.

items.summary: Plazza expects to expand to other cities, and is gunning for over 3,000 stores in the next three to four years.

items.summary: The move is aimed at creating opportunities for employment, entrepreneurship, apprenticeship, higher education, reskilling and international certifications of the candidates.

items.title: JPC weighs key changes to Corporate Laws Bill, reviews AIF-to-LLP conversion and CSR norms
items.summary: A parliamentary panel is examining changes to corporate law amendments. Investors may become partners in newly formed LLPs instead of trustees. The committee also debates proposed relaxations in corporate social responsibility norms. A framework for overseas companies to re-domicile in India is under consideration. The panel expects to adopt its report in early August for parliamentary review.

items.title: Thames Water lenders preparing legal challenge in event of Burnham nationalisation
items.summary: The BBC understands creditors would would pursue payment in full of the outstanding debts, in the multi-billions of pounds.

items.summary: The incoming PM is expected to announce fresh support for the North Sea from day one of his premiership.

items.title: Govt eases battery PLI norms to attract more storage players; subsidies lowered
items.summary: The new 10 GWh tender, the final part of the larger 50 GWh initiative under the ₹18,100-crore production-linked incentive (PLI) scheme for advanced chemistry cells (ACC), was floated on 15 July and marks a shift in policy priorities, according to tender documents reviewed by Mint.

items.title: Who's really making money from the AI boom? Follow the cash behind OpenAI, Nvidia & Big Tech
items.summary: The AI boom has triggered massive spending, with companies investing heavily in infrastructure, startups, and data centers. However, uncertainties persist regarding the flow of investments and the industry’s sustainability, particularly if major players like OpenAI were to fail.

items.title: Reliance Retail plans to expand online business and dark stores in FY27, CFO Dinesh Taluja lays out three-year roadmap
items.summary: Reliance Retail plans to ‘rapidly’ scale its online business, expand dark stores and strengthen omni-channel capabilities, CFO Dinesh Taluja told PTI. The company plans to evaluate markets and focus investments accordingly, he added.

items.title: Piramal Finance bets on in-house AI to cut costs, boost efficiency
items.summary: Piramal Finance is trying to use AI to save money, not to spend even more.

items.title: ASEAN, Africa lead India's export growth in first two months of FY27
items.summary: ASEAN and Africa were the biggest drivers of India's export growth in the first two months of FY27, with exports to ASEAN surging 66.9% to $10.5 billion and shipments to Africa rising 53.1% to $9.6 billion, according to commerce ministry data. Together, the two regions contributed over $7.6 billion in additional exports during April-May.

items.title: Anger as water supply misery continues for second day
items.summary: Residents in Kent describe South East Water as "incompetently useless" as supply issues continue.

items.title: 'Dr. Doom' Nouriel Roubini predicts 10% economic growth by 2050—but says AI will replace many jobs
items.summary: Economist Nouriel Roubini says AI and robotics could replace much of the workforce, making universal basic income inevitable. Here's why the 'Dr. Doom' foresees a future of 10% economic growth, AGI and wealth redistribution.

items.summary: Ahead of its planned IPO, iD Fresh is targeting best-in-class profitability, expanding into snacks and scaling new product launches as quick commerce reshapes India's ready-to-cook foods market.

items.title: Chinese firm seeks compensation over British Steel nationalisation
items.summary: Jingye said it will take action "through legal means to the very end" after the UK government nationalised British Steel.

items.title: Vice President CP Radhakrishnan, Zanzibar President Hussein Ali Mwinyi hold "wide-ranging, productive discussions" in bilateral meeting
items.summary: Vice President CP Radhakrishnan met Zanzibar President Hussein Ali Mwinyi in New Delhi. Discussions focused on strengthening trade, investment, and development partnerships between the nations. Zanzibar's President praised the new IIT Madras campus as a landmark educational initiative. External Affairs Minister S Jaishankar also met President Mwinyi to deepen bilateral ties. The visit highlights growing momentum in the India-Tanzania strategic partnership.

items.title: Samsung layoffs: Over 800 US workers affected by Texas headquarters move
items.summary: Samsung layoffs: 739 positions at Samsung Electronics America in Englewood Cliffs, New Jersey, were impacted as part of the company's plan to relocate its U.S. consumer electronics headquarters to Texas

items.title: PLI success prompts Centre to explore next phase for food processing sector
items.summary: The food processing ministry is reviewing lessons from the current PLI programme and consulting industry stakeholders before finalizing a new framework. It has also held discussions with PLI beneficiaries to identify segments requiring additional policy support.

items.title: Accenture reworks salary hikes to include more employees: Splits base pay and one-time lumpsum — Here's what changes
items.summary: Accenture has reworked its salary hike composition to include more employees in the June cycle, according to a report. The new approach aims to reward more staff while managing payroll. Here's a look at what changes…

items.title: Meta layoffs: US judge rejects bid to halt AI discrimination job cuts
items.summary: A US judge has ruled that Meta can proceed with layoffs impacting 8,000 employees, despite disputes over AI usage in selecting job cuts.

items.title: Dr Reddy's Foundation soil testing lab set to hit 1-lakh sample mark ahead of 2026 rabi season
items.summary: Dr Reddy's Foundation is expanding its soil testing operations significantly. The Hyderabad lab aims to process one lakh samples before the next Rabi season. This initiative addresses concerns about depleting soil health and government scheme implementation gaps. The foundation also partners with a startup for biological soil amendments. Collaboration with ICAR-IISS will build a digital soil health repository and advanced advisories.

items.title: Late-night Argentina-Spain Fifa final fuels business for bars, hotels and delivery apps
items.summary: Extended bar timings, packed screenings and a surge in food delivery orders are turning the Fifa World Cup final into an unexpected late-night revenue opportunity for India's hospitality industry.

items.title: Afghanistan seeks deeper trade ties with India, pitches for easier business access
items.summary: Afghanistan's trade body seeks enhanced economic ties with India. They requested easier business procedures and faster visa issuance. This comes as Afghanistan diversifies trade routes away from Pakistan. India is strengthening connectivity through Iran's Chabahar port. Both nations aim for sustainable cooperation and strengthened relations.

items.title: NITI Aayog to review industrial corridor framework, seeks reforms to speed project execution
items.summary: India's government is reviewing industrial corridor frameworks to enhance project delivery. NITI Aayog will consult stakeholders on necessary policy and regulatory changes. The review aims to identify bottlenecks and recommend reforms for industrial competitiveness. This initiative supports the nation's goal of becoming a developed country by 2047.

items.title: Deloitte projects 6.5-6.8% GDP expansion in FY27, growth to strengthen in second half
items.summary: Deloitte India on Sunday projected India's economy to grow between 6.5% and 6.8% in the current financial year, with momentum expected to pick up in the second half of FY27. The firm said growth would be supported by stronger festive demand, monetary easing and a gradual stabilisation in global economic conditions.
